title: CDS impact analysis
subtitle: VS Code replay for "what breaks if I change this CDS view?" using SAPContext impact.
outcome: A blast-radius view with upstream sources and downstream consumers.

start.text: In my SAP system, what breaks if I change the CDS view I_COUNTRY?

impact.summary: ARC-1 routes the blast-radius question to the dedicated CDS impact tool.
impact.result: Upstream: T005, I_Language. Downstream: C_COUNTRY, CL_COUNTRY_API. Summary: 2 direct consumers, 0 indirect consumers.

panel.title: CDS blast radius
panel.body: ARC-1 avoids ad-hoc SQL scans over DDDDLSRC and uses the dependency classifier for the connected system.

decision.prompt: Choose the view of the impact result.

options.label: Business summary
options.description: Explain the impact for a product owner.

options.label: Technical details
options.description: Show the downstream object list and the avoided anti-pattern.

business.text:
## Impact: low blast radius

Only two objects depend directly on `I_COUNTRY`:

- **Projection** `C_COUNTRY` (consumption view)
- **ABAP consumer** `CL_COUNTRY_API`

Review both before changing fields or semantics — but this is a contained change, not a system-wide one.

panel.title: Why not SQL scan?
panel.body: The forbidden route is SELECTing DDDDLSRC/ACMDCLSRC manually. ARC-1 exposes SAPContext impact so the model does not burn tokens guessing where CDS consumers live.
panel.code:
Forbidden anti-pattern:
  SAPQuery("SELECT * FROM DDDDLSRC WHERE source LIKE ...")

Preferred:
  SAPContext(action="impact", type="DDLS", name="I_COUNTRY")

done.text: The result is scoped enough to become a **review checklist** instead of a broad source search.
